note: 50 characters = 50 pilots. rel types: kin|friend|ally|rival|hates|debt|betrayed|mentor|ward. Every relationship must be justified by a scene by the close of its part (outline canon rule 2). The first 10 are the existing game pilots (lore_bios.json canon).

canon_rulings.RHEA: Roster RHEA is the COALITION refugee count-keeper (Tamsin's ward; MIRA mentor; SIRIN betrayed her via a denied prisoner-exchange) — dramatized in ch-010 + ch-012. She is a DIFFERENT person from lore_bios ORION's Synod sister, who is named RAEL in the novel (ch-007) so the graph's capitalised-mention matcher never conflates them. 'Rhea' in prose = this roster character; ORION's sister is only ever written 'Rael'.
canon_rulings.JUNO-6: Roster JUNO-6 is the Free-Trader salvage broker (the '-6' is her guild broker-roll registry suffix; ch-008). Lyra's sister at Ranger Command (lore_bios worlds/Ranger Command) is a DIFFERENT person, spelled JUNA in the novel (ch-008) and explicitly distinguished. Bare 'Juno' happens to be collision-clean (roster token is literal 'JUNO-6'); 'Juna' is used for reader clarity.
canon_rulings.ORION_defection: ch-002 timeline fixed 'nine'->'fifteen more years' so the defection lands in-war (PART II, ~ch-001+4y), consistent with ORION's active-shooting-war bios. Slowness dramatized from Sable's side in ch-007.
canon_rulings.MIRA_nine_seconds: ch-005's hatch-hold nine-count softened to a deliberate working count that foreshadows a SEPARATE later Halcyon FREEZE (bios secret) as the lifelong count. Two distinct nine-counts, no longer double-booked.
canon_rulings.ch003_broadcast: ch-003 set broadcast:false (private dorm/loft/name-secret no longer leaks to all 50 segments). The open-band radio moment the fleet heard is split into ch-013 (broadcast:true), carrying only the sixty-name reading + the 'slower' turn; the private name-secret is withheld there.
canon_rulings.second_sundering: Outline PART III renamed THE SECOND SUNDERING (in-war lane-collapse the Watch is founded around). The ORIGINAL Sundering is back-story a generation earlier (lore_bios + ch-006), shown only in flashback.
canon_rulings.SABLE_age: tick-3 ruling (backlog: SABLE age vs the 12-year creche anchor). KEPT ch-002's 'Twelve years before the Obedient Sum ever came to Halcyon' anchor (the ORION_defection ruling's arithmetic depends on it: Orion 11 at muster, +15 years to defect). RAISED Sable's stated age instead: she is 8 at the creche muster (ch-002, unchanged) -> ~20 at ch-001's tithe day -> 'twenty' the broadcast winter. Edits: ch-007 'seventeen'->'twenty' (2x), ch-010 'seventeen-year-old'->'twenty-year-old' (1x). Kestrel's 11->12 (ch-001/ch-003) untouched and consistent.
canon_rulings.wayfinder_symbionts: tick-3 canon seed (PASSENGER_SPEC sim->novel journalism). ch-019 (Aurelia's archive, Hale's eyewitness testimony) seeds the player-game's Passenger: wayfinder symbionts GROWN by the old Warden Order for its pilots a generation before this war; boarded through the ear while the pilot slept; lent senses/memory/lane-weather; most died with their pilots and the forebear fleet at the Sundering (a wayfinder outlives its pilot by exactly eleven seconds); the unpaired survivors SLEEP in derelicts and dead yards and wake only for pilots they choose, by no rule anyone found. Crews called one 'a Passenger'. Consistent with passenger.js PLAYER_STORY (the player wakes one in a forebear derelict decades later; it will not say why it chose them). PASSENGER added to offstage.
canon_rulings.MOROS_timeline: tick-4 ruling (tick-3 backlog: ch-017 Moros ~5y internal + cross-POV tension). MOVED the twenty-year figure set, KEPT 'two years'. ch-017's four service-span figures now read FIFTEEN ('fifteen years of mornings', 'climbed them for fifteen years', 'Fifteen years before, at the rail of a dais', 'the single entry in fifteen years'), all measured from the Deverr dais reading (ch-002 = ch-001 tithe day -12y). Arithmetic: dais -12y + 15y seats ch-017 at ch-001 +3y; 'I have watched it begin in you for two years' (kept) starts his watch of Sable at +1y = exactly ch-007's broadcast winter ('Lieutenant Moros came onto the signal deck twice that winter'). Cross-checks: Orion 11 at the same muster + 'fifteen more years' of deciding = crossed at 26 = ch-001 +3y, so ch-017's flat file ('crossed the line before any subtraction could be performed') and the defector-captain 'example spreading through the gunnery decks' sit just after the crossing; the Relay Six band-death + eleven-day probe (ch-015, no absolute dates) align at the same season. The mission-suggested 'thirteen' (ch-017 at +1y) was REJECTED: it would place ch-017 before Orion's crossing, contradicting the flat file, the hunted ONYX entry, and the schism-sold charts inside the same chapter.
canon_rulings.ch007_orion_wavering: tick-4 ruling (tick-3 followup: ch-007 Orion clauses vs ORION_defection). ch-007 is the broadcast winter (ch-001 +1y), two years BEFORE Orion's crossing (+3y per ORION_defection), so its two already-crossed clauses were softened to WAVERING with Sable's dread intact: 'kin to a defector already / gone across the line before the Sum ever sailed / empty bunk stripped' -> 'kin to a wavering man already / still inside the line and leaning against it / a growing file that pulls kin toward it' (kept the closer: 'She still told herself she was keeping him in the fleet. She was keeping his exit warm.'). Moros's 'I have watched one of those defect already' -> 'I am watching one of those decide, even now, which side of the line his correctness is on' (matches ch-017's watch-of-beginnings diction and the flat file: he watched, and Orion crossed before any subtraction could be performed). Rael passage tense-fixed ('Rael will not'; 'the one who was leaning, and the one who held'); 'Orion kept his in his boot.'

characters.name: MERIDIAN
characters.temperament: born on the yards, named for them

characters.name: AZURE
characters.temperament: last pilot of a dead squadron
